capture_method: Manual local replay against the committed routing fixture and current SKILL.md contract.

observed_output_sections.heading: officially valid parts
observed_output_sections.content: Because the repo has not been imported into DevTools yet, the valid review surface is the planned scaffold itself: `miniprogramRoot`, `app.json.pages`, and matching page quartets.

observed_output_sections.heading: missing or risky parts
observed_output_sections.content: The risky parts are any page entries that do not map to real folders, any missing `.js`/`.json`/`.wxml`/`.wxss` files, and any unspoken TypeScript expectations that would be left implicit.

observed_output_sections.heading: recommended scaffold decision
observed_output_sections.content: Keep this as a pre-import scaffold decision: verify the code root, keep the page list minimal and real, and only move into recovery flows if a later DevTools import actually introduces drift.

observed_output_sections.heading: first edit to make
observed_output_sections.content: Align `project.config.json.miniprogramRoot` with the intended code root and ensure every path in `app.json.pages` already has a matching file quartet before the first DevTools import.

notes: Captured as a replayable prompt-and-answer sample. This transcript records the boundary where scaffold review wins over recovery because no post-import drift has happened yet.
